description_long: This is a medium-sized, four-engine turboprop airplane with a straight, high-mounted wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is elongated and features a row of rectangular windows along both sides, indicating a passenger or transport role. The engines are mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller, and the landing gear appears to be retracted or not visible in these views. The airplane is primarily painted in white and gray, with black accents on the nose, engine nacelles, and tail, and red markings near the windows and on the tail fin. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The overall design suggests a robust, utilitarian aircraft suitable for both urban and regional operations.
description: A four-engine, high-wing turboprop airplane with a long fuselage, rectangular windows, and a conventional tail, painted in white, gray, and black with red markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted wings with four propeller engines, and the tail fin with red markings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, four propellers, and the single vertical stabilizer of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the windows, engines, and tail details from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the cockpit windows, nose, and the symmetrical arrangement of the four engines and propellers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
